The inequality $2x^3 - 5x^2 + 3x - 7 \ge 10$ simplifies to $2x^3 - 5x^2 + 3x - 17 \ge 0$. Evaluating the function $f(x) = 2x^3 - 5x^2 + 3x - 17$ at the endpoints of the interval $[3, 6]$: $f(3) = 54 - 45 + 9 - 17 = 1$ and $f(6) = 432 - 180 + 18 - 17 = 253$. Since the derivative $f'(x) = 6x^2 - 10x + 3$ is positive for all $x \ge 3$ (as the roots are approximately 0.4 and 1.3), the function is strictly increasing on this interval. Because the minimum value on $[3, 6]$ is $1$, which is greater than or equal to $0$, the inequality holds for the entire range. Thus, the range of x-values satisfying the condition is $3 \le x \le 6$.

To find the screen width where the area is 15 square inches, we solve $2x^3 - 5x^2 + 3x - 7 = 15$, which simplifies to $2x^3 - 5x^2 + 3x - 22 = 0$. Testing integer values, we find that $x = 3$ yields $2(27) - 5(9) + 3(3) - 22 = 54 - 45 + 9 - 22 = -4$, which is not zero.
